events.title: Brooklyn SeltzerFest
events.description: After a sold-out debut that drew more than 600 fizzy fanatics, Brooklyn SeltzerFest is returning to Industry City on March 15, 2026—and it’s expanding into a full-day celebration of New York’s most iconic bubbly. Last year, the New York Post hailed the festival as a gathering of “bubbling… fizzy fans” honoring a “quirky century-old tradition” at the heart of NYC’s cultural lifeblood. Eater spotlighted the now-legendary National Egg Cream Invitational, judged by filmmaker Benny Safdie, and praised the festival’s joyful blend of history, flavor, and nostalgia. Now, the second annual Brooklyn SeltzerFest is set to go even bigger.

events.title: CREA Drop Off Adventure
events.description:
CREA’s Drop-Off Adventure is a 60-minute immersive experience that combines technology with traditional play in a sensory-rich environment. Kids are guided through interactive adventures following a light sensor pathway to each new zone. The entire environment – from floors to walls – is alive with projected wall-sized motion controlled games including a glowing cube puzzle challenge, a rotating rockwall, a physioball bouncing pool, and a meditation area. Kids are inspired to work together in mixed-age teams to achieve a group score.
Every Saturday and Sunday 12-6 pm
All of our games are STEAM-inspired, encourage play-based learning and team building and feature themes about the environment made in collaboration with our students in CREA’s Design Lab.
Book ahead through the link below for an unforgettable experience!
https://creainteractivity.com/
CREA also offers 2 hour weekend adventures any time slot from 12-6pm. Start with a one-hour guided immersive experience, then choose your own path: Keep exploring with an extra hour of open play or get hands-on in our Design & Discovery Lab

-Let your creativity flow and take home your handmade jewelry!
Come craft with us and create something truly special!

events.title: Origami Workshop for Beginners
events.description:
This beginner-friendly origami workshop is open to all levels, with clear, step-by-step guidance.
Participants will learn basic folding techniques and occasionally create seasonal-themed models such as flowers, hearts, and decorative ornaments.
All materials are included.
The workshop is $20 per person for a 30-minute session, with extension options available upon request.

events.title: Sunday Storytime: To Boldly Go by Angela Dalton with Special Guests from the New York City Star Trek Meetup
events.description:
As Lieutenant Uhura on the iconic prime-time television show Star Trek, Nichelle Nichols played the first Black female astronaut anyone had ever seen on-screen. A smart, strong, independent Black woman aboard the starship Enterprise was revolutionary in the 1960s, when only white men had traveled to outer space in real life and most Black characters on TV were servants.
Nichelle not only inspired a generation to pursue its dreams but also opened the door for the real-life pioneering astronauts Sally Ride, Dr. Mae Jemison, and more. Join us at Powerhouse @ IC for a special storytime featuring photo ops with visitors from the Enterprise (the New York City Star Trek Meetup Group, as seen at the USS Intrepid, ABC-TV’s Good Morning America, and more), coloring sheets, and other surprises!
events.vendor: Sunday Storytime: To Boldly Go by Angela Dalton with Special Guests from the New York City Star Trek Meetup

events.title: New Love: A Play by Adam Szymkowicz
events.description:
brooklynONE productions Presents the North American Premiere of
New Love
by Adam Szymkowicz
Directed by Christy Hall | March at the Tom Kane Theatre
brooklynONE productions will present the North American premiere of New Love, the internationally produced play by Adam Szymkowicz, this March at the Tom Kane Theatre in Industry City.
Told through a fast-moving series of scenes set across New York City—from bars and bedrooms to rehearsal halls and public spaces—New Love is a smart, funny, and deeply human exploration of what it means to pursue connection while carrying the weight of past relationships, professional uncertainty, and the fear of not being “enough.”
This production culminates in a live audience choice that determines the play’s final moments. Rather than functioning as a novelty, the moment invites the audience to step into the same emotional crossroads faced by the characters, reinforcing the play’s central question: when connection feels risky, do we choose safety or vulnerability?
Brianna Espinal and Isaiah Rothstein, both brooklynONE company members, bring depth, humor, and emotional honesty to their roles of Suze and Miles, continuing brooklynONE’s commitment to actor-driven storytelling and contemporary voices.
Founded in Brooklyn, and now in its 20th Season, brooklynONE productions is a 501(c)(3) not-for-profit performing arts and events company dedicated to producing accessible, adventurous theatre while supporting artists at every stage of their careers. Known for its DIY spirit and ambitious programming, brooklynONE has become a vital hub for new work and community-centered storytelling in New York City.
New Love runs March 5,6,7,8,12,13,14,15 at bkONE: The Tom Kane Theatre at Industry City. Tickets are on sale now at www.bkone.org/tix.
events.vendor: New Love: A Play by Adam Szymkowicz

events.title: Warm Sake Tasting Class and Brewery Tour
events.description:
For a long time, warm sake has gotten a bad rap! But it can be absolutely delicious when you pick the right sake and the right temperature. Join us for this exciting exploration of sake temperature and the joys of warming sake. It’s the perfect way to explore the subtle umami and richness of premium sake. We’ll contrast some chilled sake and taste the same sake warm as well and enjoy some carefully selected food pairings to round out the tasting flight. In all, we’ll taste 5 kinds of sake, each paired with a delicious bite to bring out the flavors and textures.
As always, this class will include a behind the scenes brewery tour to see how sake is made at NY State’s first sake brewery.
What’s included:
A welcome sake to get you started!
A brief intro to sake temperature.
Guided Tasting of 5 craft sakes served at different temperatures to bring out a unique nuance in the sake.
Exploration of sake production styles and flavors.
Enjoy some nibbles with each sake to enjoy a curated pairing.
Handouts so you can bring your newfound sake temperature knowledge home
Finish with a Guided tour through our new Sake Brewery
events.vendor: Warm Sake Tasting Class and Brewery Tour

events.title: Private Industry City Tour
events.description:
Embark on a captivating behind-the-scenes journey through Industry City, Brooklyn’s hub of creativity and innovation, and be part of a transformative experience that connects you deeply with the cultural and economic heartbeat of this dynamic locale. Our expertly curated tour not only tantalizes your senses but also actively engages you in the meaningful revitalization of this historic area. Let us guide you through an immersive exploration that promises to be as enriching as it is entertaining.
Private tour available Thursday, Fridays and Sundays at 2pm-5pm.
Meet your guide 5-10 minutes before the tour starts on the corner of 36th Street & 3rd Ave, Brooklyn, NY 11232. You’ll meet your guide near the citibikes outside of Japan Village.
Pricing starts at $550 for a group of 4 and include food, drinks and the maker appointments.

events.description: Join Puff Media at Barrow’s Intense NY Tasting Room for an exclusive Cosplay Night celebrating the Season 2 premiere of the live-action One Piece. While One Piece attire is encouraged, cosplayers of all genres and skill levels are welcome to enjoy the festivities, participate in raffles, and connect with the community in a vibrant, free-entry setting.

events.title: Women in Art Group Show
events.description:
brooklynONE productions presents Women in Art, a vibrant group exhibition celebrating original works created by women artists who are boldly expressing themselves and their creative vision.
This evening brings together a dynamic range of styles, mediums, and perspectives in one powerful, colorful gallery experience. From intimate personal narratives to striking visual statements, each piece reflects the individuality and artistic voice of its creator. Expect conversation, community, and work that sparks something real.
Join us at The Tom Kane Theatre in Industry City for an 8PM reception, meet the artists, connect with fellow creatives, and support independent art in Brooklyn.
$10 RSVP
Your RSVP directly supports brooklynONE productions and helps us continue producing independent theatre, exhibitions, and community-driven arts programming throughout the year.

events.title: Rye Revolution Distillery Tour
events.description:
Rye Revolution Tour
1 hour @ $30.00
Join Fort Hamilton Distillery for an exciting new distillery tour! On this one hour tour, guests will learn what it takes to start a craft distillery, and hear the fascinating history of Rye whiskey, Fort Hamilton’s specialty. You’ll also learn the ins and outs of whiskey production and barrel ageing, and have a chance to bottle whiskey as well as taste it straight from the barrel. Tour includes a tasting of four Whiskeys, Gin & Barrel aged Maple Syrup.

events.title: Wine & Spirits Tastings Every Saturday!
events.description: Sahadi Spirits is hosting a wine or spirit tasting every Saturday from 4-6PM from now until the end of the year! Stop by to try something new! Weekly Insta posts on @sahadispirits will show what the upcoming tasting will be.

events.title: Industry City Gourmet Food and Drinks Tour
events.description:
Set against the iconic backdrop of Sunset Park’s historic waterfront, the massive, revitalized structures of Industry City form a breathtaking stage for an exclusive culinary journey. This premium food and cocktail tour is a curated exploration of taste and craft, transforming an evening into a memorable, high-end experience.
Your adventure begins with artistic bonbons at Lilac Chocolates, explores gourmet imports at Sahadi’s, and includes a refined whiskey tasting at Fort Hamilton Distillery, followed by a sophisticated sake flight. Savor additional artisanal discoveries within the awe-inspiring industrial halls.
Perfect as a distinctive date night, this tour is also fully customizable for private groups and corporate retreats. We specialize in tailoring this engaging experience for team-building and client entertainment, making it an ideal one-day corporate outing. A sophisticated mini-vacation for locals and a profound discovery for visitors.
events.vendor: Industry City Gourmet Food and Drinks Tour

events.title: Guided Brewery Tour and Sake Tasting
events.description:
Join us for a tour of our state-of-the-art sake brewery in Industry City, Brooklyn. See first hand how we turn rice and water into delicious Brooklyn-made premium craft sake. Led by our expert brewery staff, this experience will walk you through our production facility and allow you to take in the sights, sounds and delicious smells of New York’s premier sake brewery. The tour concludes in our taproom where we will lead you a guided tasting flight of 3 kinds of Brooklyn Kura and introduce you to the nuance and variety of our award-winning sake.
Location:
Please arrive promptly in our taproom to start your tour. The location is:
34 34th Street, Inner courtyard
Industry City Building 6
Brooklyn, NY 11232
events.vendor: Guided Brewery Tour and Sake Tasting
